headlines.title: Global Refiners Are Cutting Out Oil Traders To Buy Venezuelan Crude Directly
headlines.summary: Commodity traders are having the rug pulled out from under one of their biggest paydays yet as refiners start bypassing oil and traders and buying Venezuelan crude directly, according to Reuters. Refiners and major oil-producing firms are rapidly gaining market share in Venezuela

headlines.title: New Pipelines Set To Ease Permian Natural Gas Glut
headlines.summary: The regional price of natural gas produced in the Permian, the top U.S. oil basin, was negative for most of the first half of the year. headlines.title: Houthi Red Sea Blockade Could Shatter Hopes for Lower Oil Prices

headlines.title: Africa’s Richest Man Proposes To Build 700,000 Bpd Oil Refinery In Kenya
headlines.summary: East Africa has spent decades exporting one commodity and importing another. headlines.title: Brazil’s Oil Boom Is Accelerating as Asian Buyers Flee the Middle East
headlines.summary: Since Middle East hostilities reignited, oil prices have soared higher, although they remain below the highs witnessed during April 2026. This can be blamed on U.S. strikes against Iran, with Tehran and Washington disrupting traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, through which a fifth

headlines.title: LNG Supply Crisis Pushes Buyers Toward Coal and Oil
headlines.summary: The resumption of the Iran conflict, including the effective closures of the Strait of Hormuz and now the Strait of Bab el-Mandeb has driven up natural gas prices, particularly in Europe and Asia. Asia accounts for nearly 90% of liquefied natural gas (LNG) shipments from key Midd

headlines.title: US Oil, Product Inventories See Builds Across the Board
headlines.summary: Crude oil inventories in the United States saw an increase of 2.0 million barrels during the week ending July 17, according to new data from the U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) released on Wednesday. The increase brings commercial stockpiles to 411.7 million barrels,

headlines.title: Iran War Escalation Threatens Global Fuel Supply Recovery
